Transcrição Completa: 14 Section 7.2 - Quarterly Objectives Planning
[00:00] Welcome back. Section 7.2. At this time, you better have your one-page attack and conquer plan open. Again, we've knocked out market leader positioning, your who, what, and why, your niche tagline, and we've set your annual goals.
[00:17] Now, it's time to take those macro annual goals and break it down into quarterly objectives. Before we get into that, I want to go over something that's critical, okay?
[00:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Without a date, nothing is real"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Texto enfático destacado, possivelmente em negrito ou com design de alerta para chamar atenção sobre a importância de prazos
[00:30] Without a date, nothing is real. Okay, let me be clear. In anything that we do moving forward, you must have a date. Without a date, you can never hold anybody accountable to anything.
[00:43] The second piece is as you start to assign ownership, you need one neck to choke. One person ultimately responsible of what we're going to get into in these quarterly objectives.
[00:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "One Neck to Choke - Accountability Structure"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Diagrama hierárquico mostrando uma pessoa no topo (responsável principal) com possíveis membros de equipe abaixo, ilustrando cadeia de responsabilidade
[00:57] And they can have people operate underneath them that are responsible of things. But you need to be able to go to one person and say like Jay's on camera right now. If I'd say Jay, this is due on this date. You can get your team to work on it. I'm going to go to Jay and Jay better have my stuff done on that date. And that's my neck to choke. Make sense?
[01:20] All right. Now, let's open up the one-page attack and conquer plan and let's look at these actions.
[01:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "One-Page Attack and Conquer Plan - Quarterly Objectives Section"
NÚMEROS: 4-6 objectives per quarter
VISUAL: Template de planejamento com seções divididas, área destacada para objetivos trimestrais, layout limpo e organizado para preenchimento
[01:30] Now, in the template here, nothing changes annually on this sheet once it's filled out besides the quarterly objectives. All right, the quarterly objectives are the, you know, four to five to six things that you believe are critical sub micro task that will help you achieve that annual objective, right? The profit and the revenue.
[01:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quarterly Objectives Example - Digital Agency"
NÚMEROS: 5 objectives listed, 25% profit margin, 30% growth target
VISUAL: Lista numerada ou com bullet points mostrando os cinco objetivos principais em formato de exemplo, com destaque para métricas de performance
[01:58] So, in an example here, I'm using a previous digital agency. One of those objectives would be:
Create and implement a marketing strategy and plan
Create and implement sales strategy and plan
Create and implement an organic growth strategy and plan
Optimize operations and delivery to ensure we never drop below 25% profit
Create and implement account retention strategy and plan
[02:26] Okay. So, if you went back to my three major goals and actions, 30% profit, 25— I mean 30% growth, 25 plus percent profit, no customer loss. I've now mapped those in my quarterly objectives, right?
[02:43] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Mapping Quarterly to Annual Goals"
NÚMEROS: 30% growth, 25%+ profit, 0% customer loss
VISUAL: Diagrama de conexão mostrando como objetivos trimestrais específicos se conectam aos três grandes objetivos anuais, possivelmente com setas ou linhas conectoras
[02:45] Create and implement a marketing strategy and plan that's going to help growth and profitability. Implement a sales strategy and plan, growth and profitability. Create an organic growth strategy and plan profitability. Optimize operations to ensure we never go below it. Right? Metrics in place that give me indicators and then create and implement an account retention strategy and plan. That's the no customer loss.
[03:11] You see how I've mapped those back to the annual. So, this is key. And these are still big things, right? I mean, building out a marketing strategy, implementing a marketing strategy plan, that's a big objective, right?
[03:27] You've got to build it and then start executing against that. But that's going to be needed in order for us to hit the revenue and profitability goals.
[03:35] So once you map those, and this needs to be specific to what's going on in your business, right? I mean, feel free to use these. I mean, they're pretty needed across any agency or any company, but you may have something more specific based on your annual goals.
[03:51] But these can't be too tactical. They have to be somewhat high level, and the tactical stuff goes below it.
[03:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Assign Ownership - Leadership Team Structure"
NÚMEROS: N/A
VISUAL: Template com colunas para objetivos e espaços para nomes de responsáveis, estrutura organizacional simples mostrando atribuição de tarefas
[04:00] Once you build this out, now you have to assign an owner. So depending on what size, if you're a one-man show right now or just a couple people, it'll be your name and your couple team members. If you're running a larger organization, these names really should be the different people in your leadership team that go here.
[04:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Workshop Time - Action Steps"
NÚMEROS: 10-15 minutes, 90 days (quarterly period)
VISUAL: Ícone de relógio ou timer, checklist de ações a tomar, design motivacional para iniciar o exercício prático
[04:22] So with that being said, it's [clears throat] workshop time. Make sure you have your one-page attack and conquer plan. Really look at those annual goals, your revenue, your profit, you know, if you included another one like in my example, no customer loss, then fill out what are the big bucket activities you have to achieve that quarter, right?
[04:46] In 90 days or less to then roll up to measure every day, which we're going to talk about on how you hit that annual goal.
[04:54] All right, take 10, 15 minutes to think this through. It shouldn't take any longer than that. Set your iPhone timer and get going. And I'll see you in the Q&A.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
4 a 6 objetivos trimestrais recomendados
25%+ margem de lucro alvo
30% meta de crescimento
0% perda de clientes
90 dias ou menos por período trimestral
10-15 minutos para completar o exercício
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
One-Page Attack and Conquer Plan (plano de ataque e conquista de uma página)
Quebra de objetivos anuais em objetivos trimestrais
Sistema de responsabilização "one neck to choke" (uma pessoa responsável)
Mapeamento de objetivos trimestrais para metas anuais
Cinco pilares estratégicos exemplo:
Marketing strategy and plan
Sales strategy and plan
Organic growth strategy and plan
Operations optimization
Account retention strategy
ESTRUTURA:
Hierarquia de planejamento: Anual → Trimestral → Diário
Estrutura de responsabilidade: Um líder principal com equipe subordinada
Framework de objetivos: Alto nível (estratégico) vs Tático (operacional)
Componentes do plano: Market leader positioning → Who/What/Why → Niche tagline → Annual goals → Quarterly objectives
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Meta de lucro: 25%+
Meta de crescimento: 30%
Foco em receita e lucratividade simultâneas
Otimização operacional para manter margens
DURAÇÃO: ~5 minutos FORMATO: Aula em vídeo com apresentação de slides e exercício prático (workshop)
